No other appearance by any party or counsel.

The parties stipulate that the matter can be heard by Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead.

Matter called, no argument is held.

Ruling: There being no other appearance and no argument held, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Certain Underwriters at Lloyd's Motion For Protective Order as follows: The Lloyd's Underwriters' motion for a protective order is denied. The Underwriters have not shown that the protections afforded by the stipulated protective order already in place in this action are inadequate to meet the Underwriters' concerns. Plaintiffs are to coordinate their production of materials designated confidential by the Underwriters in the Lloyd's action to ensure Lloyd's an adequate opportunity to designate such materials under the protective order in this action, without delaying timely production as required under the order on Swett & Crawford's motions. Order signed.
